  • Dial in MCP tools — verify all tools working, clean up stale ones

  • Plugin polish — hooks, commands, agent templates up to date

  • Plugin update + reinstall after any changes

Tomorrow (Sunday) — Content Pipeline

  • Content pipeline end-to-end review

  • Jon / Cindi flow testing — create_content, HITL gates, export

  • Content scheduling & distribution automation

  • First real content pieces through the pipeline

Setup Google Cloud

The real work happens in Google Cloud Console (separate from Admin Console):

1. Go to console.cloud.google.com

2. Create a new project (e.g. "protoLabs Studio")

3. APIs & Services → Library → Enable these 3:

- Gmail API

- Google Calendar API

- Cloud Pub/Sub API (for webhook push notifications)

4. APIs & Services → Credentials → Create OAuth 2.0 Client ID:

- Application type: Web application

- Authorized redirect URI: http://localhost:3008/api/auth/google/callback

(for local dev)

- Save the Client ID and Client Secret

5. OAuth consent screen → Set to Internal (since you have Workspace, this

skips Google review)

Once you have the Client ID + Secret, drop them in Discord or let me know —

we'll wire them into the Google Workspace MCP server

(taylorwilsdon/google_workspace_mcp) which has 100+ tools for Calendar + Gmail

out of the box.
